celebration: 🎉🎉 SHEEP'S NAME-DAY — held as a promise since Week 27, the class can spell S-H-E-E-P at last (sh + ee + p). Bee debuts, decodable at its very first appearance (b + ee). ee/ea is the biggest single harvest of the whole program (+75). And the ship/sheep pair is now sayable — the seed of the Week-50 EAL review.

songs.title: Ee-Ee-Tree!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(ee… ee… ee…)
Ee! Ee! Tree!

[Hook]
Double E — see! (I can see!)
E-A — sea! (down by the sea!)
Ee-ee-tree! Ee-ee-tree!
Two letters, one sound! (ee!)

[Verse 2]
S-E-E — see! (see the bee!)
T-R-E-E — tree! (up the tree!)
Ee-ee-tree! Ee-ee-tree!
The E says its name! (ee!)

[Verse 3]
S-H-E-E-P — Sheep! (that's the name!)
B-E-E — Bee! (hello, Bee!)
Ee-ee-tree! Ee-ee-tree!
Sheep can READ! (Sheep can read!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No ee!
(ee… ee… ee…)

[Outro]
See… tree… read… (shhh…)
Ee! Ee! Tree!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ee and ea both sing long-e /iː/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'ee', printed ee/ea). Verse 3 is the NAME-DAY: spell S-H-E-E-P out loud as the emotional peak — this is the payoff of a promise made in Week 27 — and welcome Bee (B-E-E). 'Sheep can READ' is the triumph line. Spell-outs uppercase. Potato has no ee. Build verse 3 to a big warm choir hook.

songs.title: Sheep Can Read!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Sheep can read! Sheep can read!
S-H-E-E-P — Sheep!
Sheep can read! Sheep can read!
(That's the name! That's the name!)

[Verse 2]
The bee is in the tree!
Sheep can see the bee!
Sheep can read! Sheep can read!
The bee can read too — wait… (a bee can read?!)

[Verse 3]
Eat at the tree! (yum! yum!)
See the sea! (the deep blue sea!)
Sheep can read! Sheep can read!
Sheep and Bee can read!

[Outro]
The tree… the bee… (shhh…)
At the sea?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Sheep can read!' — the whole song is a name-day celebration. The hook spells S-H-E-E-P (uppercase letter names). Bee debuts. Warm, proud, joyful — the biggest hearted moment since Snake's name-day at Week 38. Whisper the outro; potato hits loud at the sea.

spreads.artNote: A great friendly tree alone in the spotlight, leaves catching the light. A gathering place.
CAST DEBUT: Bee — a plump little honeybee with soft fuzzy gold-and-black stripes and round shiny wings — hovering in the spotlight, decodable at its very first appearance (b + ee).

spreads.text: The bee is in the tree.
spreads.artNote: Bee tucked into a blossom high in the friendly tree, wings a blur.
Sheep — fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — gazing up at Bee in the tree, hoof shading its eyes.

THE NAME-DAY MOMENT. Sheep stands beside a big banner spelling S-H-E-E-P in gold letters, reading its OWN name for the first time in 44 weeks. The class reads it aloud. A friend since Week 27, spellable at last.
Sheep sitting with an open book, hoof on the page, eyes wide with wonder — the payoff. Bee peeks over its shoulder.
Bee perched on the corner of the page, reading along, tiny reading glasses on its nose. Comic delight.
Sheep and Bee sharing a picnic under the tree — a slice of green-apple, a drop of honey. Friendship.
The scene opens to the shore: a single potato sits on the sand reading a book by the deep blue sea, tiny sunglasses on. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Sheep and Bee stare from the dunes.

sentences: The bee is in the tree.
Sheep and Bee eat at the sea.
extras: THE NAME-DAY CEREMONY: this is the promise the class was made in Week 27 come true. Spell S-H-E-E-P together, out loud, and hang Sheep's name beside the ee/ea leaf on the Pattern Tree — the class can spell a friend who has been with them for 17 weeks. Make it as big as Snake's name-day.
TWO SPELLINGS, ONE SOUND: ee (see, tree, feet — the reliable one) and ea (sea, eat, read — has exceptions, but says /iː/ here). Sort picture cards into two baskets.
WELCOME BEE — decodable at debut (b + ee). Bee joins the cast on the very week its name becomes spellable.

EAL — THE BIG PAIR: sheep vs ship, feet vs fit, seat vs sit. Long-e /iː/ (tense) vs short-i /ɪ/ (lax) — Mandarin-L1 children merge these. Drill the pairs now; they return as the STARS of the Week-50 review (Ship or Sheep?).
Moveable alphabet: build s-ee, t-r-ee, s-ea, r-ea-d, and finally s-h-ee-p.

teacherFocus: THE NAME-DAY WEEK — and the biggest word-harvest of the whole program (+75). ee and ea both say long-e /iː/: ee is reliable (see, tree, feet), ea says /iː/ here (sea, eat, read) though it has exceptions later. THE CEREMONY: Sheep has been a friend since Week 27; today the class spells S-H-E-E-P for the first time — make it every bit as big as Snake's name-day, it was a promise. Welcome Bee (decodable at debut). EAL PRIORITY: sheep/ship, feet/fit, seat/sit — the tense /iː/ vs lax /ɪ/ pair Mandarin-L1 children merge. Drill it now; it stars in the Week-50 review (Ship or Sheep?).
